signs.pattern: NEVER hardcode AI model names (gpt-4, claude-3, gemini, etc) - always use environment variables or settings
Use environment variables for all API keys and secrets, never hardcode them
Always handle loading, error, and empty states for async operations

ALWAYS transform API responses from snake_case to camelCase at the boundary. Create a transform function: `const user = { userName: data.user_name, createdAt: data.created_at }`. NEVER use snake_case properties directly in TypeScript code like `data.user_name` - always map to camelCase first.
Add data-testid attributes to interactive elements for Playwright e2e tests
Never use 'any' type in TypeScript - create proper interfaces and types instead
When removing or modifying UI elements, grep for related tests and update them - stale tests that check for removed elements will fail

NEVER use import-checks as test steps (python -c 'from X import Y', hasattr, test -f). These only verify a symbol exists, not that it works. A function that raises on every call still passes an import check. Always use real behavioral tests: curl for API endpoints, pytest with actual function calls, or Playwright for UI.
signs.learnedFrom: Okta SSO PRD review — all 4 backend stories had import-check testSteps that would pass even with completely broken code

signs.pattern: This is a Hugo static site, NOT a web app with a running server. There is no API, no backend, no frontend dev server needed for verification. Test steps should only check that Hugo builds HTML files correctly (hugo --minify, test -f public/*.html). Do NOT add testUrl, MCP browser checks, Playwright verification, or frontend smoke tests. Do NOT add {config.urls.backend} or {config.urls.frontend} placeholders. The site has no running server during build.
signs.learnedFrom: TASK-001 failed 7 times because Ralph kept trying to connect to localhost for frontend smoke tests on a static site that has no server

signs.pattern: In Hugo templates, ALWAYS use .RelPermalink instead of .Permalink for CSS, JS, and page links. .Permalink generates absolute URLs using baseURL (e.g. https://elessan.ai/css/compiled.css) which breaks local development. .RelPermalink generates relative paths (e.g. /css/compiled.css) that work everywhere. The only exception is RSS feed links which need absolute URLs.
